abstract: International Higher Education (IHE) delivers expert, nuanced commentary and rigorous analysis on the evolving trends and critical challenges facing global higher education. By examining shifting funding models—from the public-good traditions of Western Europe to the market-driven privatized systems of the US and Australia—IHE explores how institutions and stakeholders navigate a complex global landscape. This publication provides essential insights into the multifaceted dimensions of internationalization, including student mobility, the expansion of branch campuses, and the persistent inequalities that shape the relationship between emerging academic systems and established global superpowers.
